name: Lac Vert Loop
name_original: Boucle de Canéjan vers le lac vert
description: Hike the moderate 5.1-mile Loop from Canéjan to Lac Vert, featuring forests, vineyards, and the serene Green Lake.
region_intro: Hiking around Canéjan offers diverse landscapes in Nouvelle-Aquitaine, southwestern France. The region is characterized by extensive pine forests, notably the Forêt Domaniale de Canéjan, and tranquil water features like Lac Vert and the Eau Bourde river. Trails also traverse picturesque vineyards, providing varied scenery for outdoor exploration. This area serves as a green lung southwest of Bordeaux, featuring gentle elevation changes suitable for a range of hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Canéjan The most popular hiking route is Lac Vert Loop, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route circles the serene Lac Vert, offering views of the lake and surrounding natural areas. Another top favourite among local hikers is Moulin de Rouillac – Green Lake loop from Canéjan, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This trail combines historical sites with natural scenery, including forests, vineyards, and the Green Lake. Local hikers also love the Green Lake – Moulin de Rouillac loop from Le Camp, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through pine forests and past the Green Lake, often completed in about 2 hours. Hiking around Canéjan is defined by dense pine forests, tranquil lakes, and scenic vineyards. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate hikes. The routes in Canéjan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Canéjan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Canéjan?
A: Hikes around Canéjan offer a diverse landscape, primarily characterized by extensive pine forests, such as the &lt;strong&gt;Forêt Domaniale de Canéjan&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll also find tranquil water features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2899316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac Vert&lt;/a&gt; and paths along the Eau Bourde river. Many trails also traverse picturesque vineyards, providing varied scenery with generally gentle elevation changes.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Canéjan?
A: Canéjan offers trails suitable for various fitness levels. You'll find &lt;strong&gt;easy routes&lt;/strong&gt;, typically up to 2 hours long with minimal elevation gain, perfect for all abilities. &lt;strong&gt;Moderate hikes&lt;/strong&gt; can extend up to 5 hours and involve more significant elevation, requiring good fitness. For more experienced hikers, there are options that are longer or involve substantial elevation gain, requiring very good fitness and sure-footedness. Overall, there are 26 easy, 9 moderate, and 2 difficult routes available.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking in Canéjan?
A: Yes, hikers can explore several natural and historical points of interest. The serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2899316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac Vert&lt;/a&gt; is a central feature for many routes, offering beautiful views. You can also discover the historic Moulin de Rouillac, which is featured on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29412479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moulin de Rouillac – Green Lake loop from Canéjan&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts the vast Forêt Domaniale de Canéjan and scenic vineyards.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Canéjan?
A: Yes, Canéjan is well-suited for circular hikes. Many trails are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e833519507/lac-vert-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac Vert Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the tranquil lake. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35540375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canéjan State Forest – Green Lake loop from Canéjan&lt;/a&gt;, offering a mix of forest and lake scenery.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Canéjan?
A: The hiking trails in Canéjan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers often praise the quiet woodlands, the serene beauty of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2899316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac Vert&lt;/a&gt;, and the well-maintained paths that offer a refreshing escape into nature. The variety of landscapes, from forests to vineyards, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Canéjan suitable for families with children?
A: Absolutely. Canéjan offers several easy routes that are ideal for families. These trails typically have minimal elevation gain and are shorter in duration, making them accessible for children. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20689628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green Lake – Green Lake loop from Le Camp&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, easy option perfect for a family outing around the lake.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Canéjan?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many natural areas in France, including forests and trails, generally permit dogs on a leash. The extensive &lt;strong&gt;Forêt Domaniale de Canéjan&lt;/strong&gt; and paths around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2899316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac Vert&lt;/a&gt; are popular spots for dog walkers. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local signage regarding pet access.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trails in Canéjan?
A: Canéjan is situated just southwest of Bordeaux, and while specific trailheads might require a short walk or local bus connection, the area is generally considered readily accessible. For detailed public transport information to specific starting points, it's advisable to check local bus schedules from Bordeaux or nearby towns.

Q: Are there any places to eat or drink near the hiking trails in Canéjan?
A: Canéjan, being a commune near Bordeaux, offers local cafes, pubs, and restaurants within the town and surrounding areas. While specific establishments directly on trailheads might be limited, you'll find options for refreshments and meals in the village center or nearby after your hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Canéjan?
A: Canéjan offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, with blooming flora or vibrant fall colors. Summer can be warm, but the dense pine forests offer welcome shade. Winter hiking is also possible, though some paths might be muddier after rain.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Canéjan?
A: There are over 35 hiking routes recorded in Canéjan on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels. This includes 26 easy routes, 9 moderate routes, and 2 more challenging trails.
tips: The Green Lake is a body of water about twenty kilometers from Bordeaux in the town of Canéjan. Fishing on this 7.5 hectare body of water is possible with a fishing license. Many species of fish are well represented in Lac Vert, including pike, smallmouth bass, carp or roach. The local fishing club regularly organizes trout releases.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Loop from Canéjan to Lac Vert trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While it requires a good level of fitness, its relatively low elevation gain of about 30 meters and mostly accessible paths make it suitable for various skill levels. It's often described as a quiet walk, enjoyable in all seasons.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse mix of surfaces. You'll encounter hiking trails, unpaved paths, and natural surfaces, along with some sections of stone slabs, asphalt, and roads. Some parts are easily accessible, while others may require sure-footedness. The trail is generally well-maintained and passes through shaded woodlands.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations are not detailed, the trail is easily accessible from Canéjan. As it's a loop, you can typically find parking near the starting point in the town or designated areas around the Forêt communale de Canéjan.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features will I encounter on this hike?
faqs.answer: Hikers can expect diverse and pleasant scenery, alternating between tranquil forests, picturesque vineyards, and views of Green Lake (Lac Vert). The area around the lake is particularly scenic, and the route is known for its blend of natural and cultivated landscapes.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The central feature of this loop is the beautiful Green Lake (Lac Vert), which offers a picturesque setting. The trail itself provides varied landscapes, moving between woodlands and vineyards, making the entire journey an engaging experience.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is part of or intersects with other local trails. You'll find sections that follow official paths such as Chemin de l'étameur, Chemin de l'eau Bourde, Chemin du résinier, Chemin du Jurat, and Chemin du louvetier, among others. It also connects to parts of the larger "Parcours des Graves" network.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable in all seasons. Its relatively low elevation gain and generally accessible paths make it a good option year-round. For pleasant weather and vibrant scenery, spring and autumn are often ideal, but it's also described as a good option for a quiet walk even in winter.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Loop from Canéjan to Lac Vert is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through cultivated areas, near wildlife, or when encountering other hikers and cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Hiking the Loop from Canéjan to Lac Vert, which is located within the Forêt communale de Canéjan, typically does not require any specific permits or entrance fees for general access. However, if you plan to fish in Lac Vert, a fishing permit will be required.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restrooms near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Canéjan, so you may find amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and shops in the town itself. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your hike, as direct amenities on the trail are limited.

faqs.question: Is the Loop from Canéjan to Lac Vert a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name suggests, this is a circular route. You will start and end at the same point, making it convenient for hikers who prefer not to arrange transportation back to a different starting location.
